steps.title: Identify the four angles at the intersection
steps.content:
When two lines intersect, vertical angles are equal and adjacent angles are supplementary.

If one angle is $(7x - 260)°$, its vertical angle is also $(7x - 260)°$, and the two adjacent angles are each $180° - (7x - 260)° = (440 - 7x)°$.

Thus the four angles are $(7x-260)°, (440-7x)°, (7x-260)°, (440-7x)°$.

steps.title: Apply the constraint on x from geometry
steps.content:
For a valid geometric configuration, all angle measures must be positive. Since one angle is $(7x - 260)°$, we need $(7x - 260) > 0$, giving:
$$x > \frac{260}{7} \approx 37.14$$

steps.title: Find all possible sums of two angles
steps.content:
The four angles contain only two distinct measures. The possible sums of any two angles are:
  • Sum of two $(7x-260)°$ angles: $(14x-520)°$
  • Sum of two $(440-7x)°$ angles: $(880-14x)°$
  • Sum of one of each: $180°$
These are the only possible sums.

steps.title: Evaluate choice A
steps.content: For $x > \frac{260}{7}$, we have $14x > 520$, so $(-14x + 520) < 0$. Since the sum of two angle measures must be positive, choice A is impossible. Choices B, C, and D each match one of the sums from Step 3. Therefore, A could not be the sum of any two angles.
